content.problemStatement:
You are given two permutations (P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN)(P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N)(P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč) and (Q1,Q2,‚Ä¶,QN)(Q_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_N)(Q1‚Äč,Q2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,QN‚Äč) of values from 111 to NNN. Determine if there exists some permutation (R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RN)(R_1, R_2, \ldots, R_N)(R1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äč) of values from 111 to NNN such that the following conditions hold:


The number of inversions of permutation (PR1,PR2,‚Ä¶,PRN)(P_{R_1}, P_{R_2}, \ldots, P_{R_N})(PR1‚Äč‚Äč,PR2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PRN‚Äč‚Äč) is equal to AAA.
The number of inversions of permutation (QR1,QR2,‚Ä¶,QRN)(Q_{R_1}, Q_{R_2}, \ldots, Q_{R_N})(QR1‚Äč‚Äč,QR2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,QRN‚Äč‚Äč) is equal to BBB.
If such permutations exist, find any of them.

As a reminder, the number of inversions of permutation (P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN)(P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N)(P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč) is defined as the number of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N and Pi>PjP_i \gt P_jPi‚Äč>Pj‚Äč.


Input Format


The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.




The first line of each test case contains three integers NNN, AAA, and BBB.




The second line of each test case contains NNN integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč - the permutation PPP.




The third line of each test case contains NNN integers Q1,Q2,‚Ä¶,QNQ_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_NQ1‚Äč,Q2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,QN‚Äč - the permutation QQQ.





Output Format
For each testcase, if there is no such permutation RRR, output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1. Otherwise, output NNN integers R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RNR_1, R_2, \ldots, R_NR1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äč.
constraints: P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč form a permutation of numbers from 111 to NNN
Q1,Q2,‚Ä¶,QNQ_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_NQ1‚Äč,Q2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,QN‚Äč form a permutation of numbers from 111 to NNN
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 600060006000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have

(PR1,PR2,‚Ä¶,PRN)=(1,2,4,3)(P_{R_1}, P_{R_2}, \ldots, P_{R_N}) = (1, 2, 4, 3)(PR1‚Äč‚Äč,PR2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PRN‚Äč‚Äč)=(1,2,4,3), and has 111 inversion.
(QR1,QR2,‚Ä¶,QRN)=(4,3,1,2)(Q_{R_1}, Q_{R_2}, \ldots, Q_{R_N}) = (4, 3, 1, 2)(QR1‚Äč‚Äč,QR2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,QRN‚Äč‚Äč)=(4,3,1,2), and has 555 inversions.


Test case 222: There are no such permutations. Indeed, P=QP = QP=Q, so we can't have A‚Č†BA \neq BAÓÄ†=B.
